pattern: The effective current bit at each position only depends on how many flips have started before it, so a new flip is only ever needed exactly where the target changes value from the position before it.
hints: Instead of simulating the flips on a string, track what bit the current position would already show given the flips started so far, compared to the previous position.
Walk left to right remembering only the previous target bit (starting from an implicit '0' before the string begins).
Every time the target's bit differs from the previous one, that marks the start of a new flip; count those transitions.

edge_inputs.description: alternating bits force a flip at every index
a whole run of ones needs only one flip
mixed runs ending in a lone flipped bit
